nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to collaborate, as ive found thats the way to get high quality work

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI errors and need for correction
nodes.grounding_span: If I give the AI a task to handle independently then it gets riddled with little errors

nodes.label: AI ability to differentiate difficulty levels
nodes.label_negative: AI inability to differentiate difficulty (bigger numbers not harder)
nodes.grounding_span: i hope AIs ability to accurately determine difficulty level in maths improves

nodes.label: AI as tutor for learning and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: i dont think AI can replace tutoring. The personal connection is too vital

nodes.label: AI transparency and reproducibility
nodes.label_negative: AI flustered response when errors occur
nodes.grounding_span: when i ask the AI what happened on their end they get 'flustered' and keep unsuccessfully trying to fix the issue

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I regularly use AI for social media posts, blog posts, and email newsletter content

nodes.label: Quality of output
nodes.grounding_span: thats the way to get high quality work

nodes.label: Student learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: i need the student to be able to indentify the steps as we go through examples for themselves so they learn

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: its the repetative worksheets i will continue to get help creating

nodes.label: Satisfaction with collaborative workflow
nodes.grounding_span: I get frustrated, push though, and then am extra satisfied when the task gets done

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of error transparency
nodes.grounding_span: I do find it very frustrating when something goes wrong and i want to step back and analyse what caused the error

nodes.label: Positive expectation for AI improvement in difficulty differentiation
nodes.grounding_span: i hope AIs ability to accurately determine difficulty level in maths improves

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in tutoring decisions
nodes.grounding_span: i dont think AI can replace tutoring. The personal connection is too vital

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to collaborate, as ive found thats the way to get high quality work

nodes.label: Systematic iterative decomposition of tasks
nodes.grounding_span: work with the AI to plot out the specific subsections of that topic that would be appropriate for different levels, and then finalise one level at a time
